title: Announcing Topcoat: a framework for building full-stack reactive web apps with Rust | Tokio
excerpt: Tokio is a runtime for writing reliable asynchronous applications with Rust. It provides async I/O, networking, scheduling, timers, and more.
content_html:
Topcoat is a modular, batteries-included Rust framework for building full-stack, reactive web apps. It prioritizes simplicity and productivity. Topcoat is entirely server-rendered. Reactivity is added by rendering HTML snippets in the server and adding "reactive instructions" as metadata (similar in idea to HTMX).

Here is the "hello world" app with Topcoat:

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    topcoat::start(Router::builder().discover().build()).await.unwrap();
}

#[page("/")]
async fn home() -> Result {
    view! {
        <!DOCTYPE html>
        <html>
            <head>
                <title>"Hello world"</title>
                topcoat::dev::script()
            </head>
            <body>
                hello(name: "World")
            </body>
        </html>
    }
}

#[component]
async fn hello(name: &str) -> Result {
    view! {
        <h1>"Hello, " (name) "!"</h1>
    }
}

Getting started guide

Motivation

Before getting into more of the technical details, it is worth discussing the motivation behind Topcoat. If you saw my TokioConf talk (the one where I predicted that Rust could become a top 3 language for all green-field development), none of this will be a surprise.

Three years ago, if I told you that Rust would be a great language to build web apps with, you probably would call me crazy, and rightfully so. After all, web apps aren't traditionally performance-sensitive applications. So, the right tool for the job would be one that lets you ship fast. Performance is a nice-to-have. Unsurprisingly, the richest web app ecosystems are in languages that put a heavy focus on productivity, like JavaScript, Ruby, and PHP.

However, AI has completely reshaped that calculus. AI erases learning barriers and productivity gaps. The difference in time it takes a modern coding AI tool to build something is primarily a factor of the available set of libraries and not the programming language. Even the operator's specific expertise becomes less important. I have watched experienced software engineers who have never written Rust before work with AI tools to build with Rust from day 1. I'm not talking about vibe coding, but using one's general-purpose engineering experience to work with an AI tool interactively to make progress while learning the language.

Now, I think Rust is a great language, and it has some really great properties (speed and reliability). However, that doesn't mean I think that you should switch to Rust if you already have a stack that works for you and you don't need Rust's performance and reliability (well, I do think you should, but I also know that is my love for Rust and not a practical suggestion). However, many organizations are adopting Rust because they have problems to solve that require a performance and reliable language. Organizations that have adopted Rust already have a lot of reason to stay with Rust for higher-level apps, not because they need the performance, but because they already have internal infrastructure (libraries, build systems, processes, ...) around that language, and minimizing the number of programming languages and tools within an organization does improve productivity.

So now, what we need to be productive with Rust for web apps is a rich library ecosystem. That is what I have been building. I started with Toasty, an ORM for Rust, because it is probably the hardest component. Toasty has been ready to use since April of 2026. The next step in the roadmap is a web framework, and that is where Topcoat comes in.

Late last year, I met Julien Scholz and was impressed by his sense of taste and passion for building a great web app framework for Rust, so I convinced him to take the time to build one: Topcoat.

Reactivity without WebAssembly

Frameworks like Leptos and Dioxus are excellent for creating highly interactive web applications using Rust by compiling your code to WebAssembly and running it in the browser. However, many applications do not require this level of interactivity. For those use cases, compiling to a separate target, worrying about bundle sizes and splitting, and serializing data across the client/server boundary become a burden. Topcoat pursues a simpler approach. All markup is rendered on the server, and so components can be async, access the database, or verify user permissions safely. To sprinkle in reactivity, it cross-compiles a subset of fully type-checked Rust expressions to JavaScript using a macro, allowing you to stay in Rust without touching WebAssembly. In this example, the <button> reveals the <p> tag below when clicked:

view! {
    // Declare a client-side state variable:
    signal open = false;
    
    <button
        // Configure a Rust closure as the "on click" handler for this button.
        // Code inside the $(...) is run as JavaScript inside the browser:
        @click=$(|_e| open.set(!open.get()))
    >
        "What is Topcoat?"
    </button>
    
    // The `hidden` attribute tracks the value of `open` and updates
    // each time the button is pressed.
    <p :hidden=$(!open.get())>"A fullstack Rust framework."</p>
}

The toggling logic runs entirely in the browser, no server round-trip needed.

Topcoat can also re-render entire parts of the UI on the server whenever client state changes, and swap out only the necessary part of the page. Here, search results are updated as the user types into the search <input> field:

#[component]
async fn search() -> Result {
    view! {
        signal query = String::new();

        // Write the current text input into the `query` signal:
        <input @input=$(|e: Event| query.set(e.target.value))>

        // Updates as the user types.
        search_results(query: $(query.get()))
    }
}

// Shards are a special type of component that exposes an API endpoint from your router.
#[shard]
async fn search_results(cx: &Cx, query: String) -> Result {
    // This function runs on the server. It can access the database asynchronously.
    view! {
        <ul>
            for product in search_products(cx, &query).await? {
                <li>(product.name)</li>
            }
        </ul>
    }
}

The client-side reactivity system is still in the early stages of development and has some limitations. We have many ideas to improve this in the future. In the meantime, you can also make use of the HTMX and Alpine.js integrations.

A complete toolset for UI development

Building web interfaces is far more complicated than rendering HTML. The web app needs to load custom fonts, stylesheets, images, and more. Topcoat offers a full asset pipeline using the asset macro:

const FERRIS: Asset = asset!("./ferris.png");

view! { <img src=(FERRIS)> }

At build time, the Topcoat CLI collects or downloads all your assets and stores them in a single directory. As your application runs, they are served using content hashes to optimize browser caching.

Every good design needs its own fonts and icons. Fontsource and Iconify offer huge libraries of freely available fonts and icons for you to use. These can be trivially included in your Topcoat app:

// Loads the "Roboto" web font from Fontsource.
const ROBOTO: Font = fontsource_font!(ROBOTO);

// Creates a Rust module containing the "feather" icon set.
iconify::include!("feather");

Coming up with a cohesive design system and all of its components can be challenging. Component libraries can get you up and running quickly, but often turn out to be inflexible as your design changes. Inspired by shadcn/ui, Topcoat's built-in component library is based on Tailwind and copies ready-made components directly into your source directory. This means you can modify anything you need and make the design truly your own:

#[component]
async fn delete_card() -> Result {
    view! {
        card(
            card_header(
                card_title("Delete workspace")
                card_description("This permanently removes the workspace and all of its data.")
            )
            card_footer(
                attrs: attributes! { class="justify-end" },
                button(variant: ButtonVariant::Ghost, "Cancel")
                button(variant: ButtonVariant::Destructive, "Delete workspace")
            )
        )
    }
}

Locality of behavior as the guiding principle

Both humans and AI are better at reasoning across small regions of code. Topcoat is architected from the ground up to allow you to keep logic local and composable. We encourage, for example, letting components do their own data fetching instead of passing data down as component arguments:

#[component]
async fn user_profile(cx: &Cx, user_id: &str) -> Result {
    // Only this component knows what user data it needs.
    let user = load_user(cx, user_id).await?;
    view! {
        <h1>(user.name)</h1>
        ...
    }
}

To avoid fetching data multiple times, Topcoat has built-in request-level memoization, inspired by React's cache:

#[memoize]
async fn load_user(cx: &Cx, user_id: &str) -> Result<User> {
    // This database call is made only once per unique `user_id`.
    db(cx).load_user_by_id(user_id).await
}

This principle can be extended further to authentication. Instead of relying on middleware in a completely different part of the codebase that may or may not run, you can protect your data directly in the component:

async fn require_auth(cx: &Cx) -> Result<User> {
    if let Some(Session { user_id }) = current_session(cx).await? {
        Ok(load_user(cx, user_id).await?)
    } else {
        // Data is kept secret, redirect to login.
        Err(redirect("/login").into())
    }
}

#[component]
async fn user_profile(cx: &Cx) -> Result {
    // `user_profile` protects itself from misuse if the user is not logged in!
    let user = require_auth(cx).await?;
    view! {
        <h1>(user.name)</h1>
        ...
    }
}

Like hooks in React (but without the dreaded rules of hooks), functions compose nicely by passing the request context (cx) around.

What about Axum

Axum is another crate hosted on tokio-rs for building server applications. Because of this, I want to call out that Topcoat and Axum cover very different use cases. In fact, I expect many Topcoat users will end up using Axum as well within their projects. Axum is a lower-level HTTP router that makes it easy to build HTTP API endpoints. You could also use Axum to build a reactive web app, but it would take a lot more boilerplate and setup. Topcoat aims to remove that boilerplate, but if you just want to build those lower-level HTTP endpoints, you should still reach for Axum.

What next

This is just the first release of Topcoat. There is a lot on our roadmap, including stuff like tighter integration with Toasty, Validations, email, etc. You can get a sense for some of it in the Readme.

In the meantime, Topcoat is ready to use now, and if you need a database, pull Toasty. Give it a try, and send us questions and/or feedback. We are all on the #topcoat channel in the Tokio Discord.

Happy building.

sentences.zh: 通过在服务器中渲染 HTML 片段并添加“反应性指令”作为元数据（与 HTMX 的思想类似）来添加反应性。

sentences.en: The next step in the roadmap is a web framework, and that is where Topcoat comes in.
sentences.zh: 路线图的下一步是 Web 框架，这就是 Topcoat 的用武之地。

blocks.html_en: Late last year, I met Julien Scholz and was impressed by his sense of taste and passion for building a great web app framework for Rust, so I convinced him to take the time to build one: Topcoat.

sentences.en: Late last year, I met Julien Scholz and was impressed by his sense of taste and passion for building a great web app framework for Rust, so I convinced him to take the time to build one: Topcoat.
sentences.zh: 去年年底，我遇到了 Julien Scholz，他的品味和为 Rust 构建出色的 Web 应用程序框架的热情给我留下了深刻的印象，所以我说服他花时间构建一个：Topcoat。

blocks.html_en: Reactivity without WebAssembly

sentences.en: Reactivity without WebAssembly
sentences.zh: 没有 WebAssembly 的反应性

blocks.html_en: Frameworks like Leptos and Dioxus are excellent for creating highly interactive web applications using Rust by compiling your code to WebAssembly and running it in the browser. However, many applications do not require this level of interactivity. For those use cases, compiling to a separate target, worrying about bundle sizes and splitting, and serializing data across the client/server boundary become a burden. Topcoat pursues a simpler approach. All markup is rendered on the server, and so components can be async, access the database, or verify user permissions safely. To sprinkle in reactivity, it cross-compiles a subset of fully type-checked Rust expressions to JavaScript using a macro, allowing you to stay in Rust without touching WebAssembly. In this example, the <button> reveals the <p> tag below when clicked:

sentences.en: Frameworks like Leptos and Dioxus are excellent for creating highly interactive web applications using Rust by compiling your code to WebAssembly and running it in the browser.
sentences.zh: 像 Leptos 和 Dioxus 这样的框架非常适合使用 Rust 创建高度交互的 Web 应用程序，方法是将代码编译为 WebAssembly 并在浏览器中运行。

sentences.en: However, many applications do not require this level of interactivity.
sentences.zh: 然而，许多应用程序不需要这种级别的交互性。

sentences.en: For those use cases, compiling to a separate target, worrying about bundle sizes and splitting, and serializing data across the client/server boundary become a burden.
sentences.zh: 对于这些用例，编译到单独的目标、担心包大小和拆分以及跨客户端/服务器边界序列化数据都成为一种负担。

sentences.en: Topcoat pursues a simpler approach.
sentences.zh: Topcoat 追求更简单的方法。

sentences.en: All markup is rendered on the server, and so components can be async, access the database, or verify user permissions safely.
sentences.zh: 所有标记都在服务器上呈现，因此组件可以异步、访问数据库或安全地验证用户权限。

sentences.en: To sprinkle in reactivity, it cross-compiles a subset of fully type-checked Rust expressions to JavaScript using a macro, allowing you to stay in Rust without touching WebAssembly.
sentences.zh: 为了增加反应性，它使用宏将经过完全类型检查的 Rust 表达式的子集交叉编译为 JavaScript，从而使您可以继续使用 Rust，而无需接触 WebAssembly。

sentences.en: In this example, the
sentences.zh: 在此示例中，单击

blocks.html_en: The toggling logic runs entirely in the browser, no server round-trip needed.

sentences.en: The toggling logic runs entirely in the browser, no server round-trip needed.
sentences.zh: 切换逻辑完全在浏览器中运行，不需要服务器往返。

blocks.html_en: Topcoat can also re-render entire parts of the UI on the server whenever client state changes, and swap out only the necessary part of the page. Here, search results are updated as the user types into the search <input> field:

sentences.en: Topcoat can also re-render entire parts of the UI on the server whenever client state changes, and swap out only the necessary part of the page.
sentences.zh: 每当客户端状态发生变化时，Topcoat 还可以在服务器上重新渲染 UI 的整个部分，并仅交换页面的必要部分。

sentences.en: Here, search results are updated as the user types into the search <input> field:
sentences.zh: 在这里，当用户在搜索 <input> 字段中键入内容时，搜索结果就会更新：

blocks.html_en: The client-side reactivity system is still in the early stages of development and has some limitations. We have many ideas to improve this in the future. In the meantime, you can also make use of the HTMX and Alpine.js integrations.

sentences.en: The client-side reactivity system is still in the early stages of development and has some limitations.
sentences.zh: 客户端反应性系统仍处于开发的早期阶段，并且存在一些局限性。

sentences.en: We have many ideas to improve this in the future.
sentences.zh: 我们有很多想法可以在未来改进这一点。

sentences.en: In the meantime, you can also make use of the HTMX and Alpine.js integrations.
sentences.zh: 同时，您还可以利用 HTMX 和 Alpine.js 集成。

blocks.html_en: A complete toolset for UI development

sentences.en: A complete toolset for UI development
sentences.zh: 用于 UI 开发的完整工具集

blocks.html_en: Building web interfaces is far more complicated than rendering HTML. The web app needs to load custom fonts, stylesheets, images, and more. Topcoat offers a full asset pipeline using the asset macro:

sentences.en: Building web interfaces is far more complicated than rendering HTML.
sentences.zh: 构建 Web 界面比渲染 HTML 复杂得多。

sentences.en: The web app needs to load custom fonts, stylesheets, images, and more.
sentences.zh: Web 应用程序需要加载自定义字体、样式表、图像等。

sentences.en: Topcoat offers a full asset pipeline using the asset macro:
sentences.zh: Topcoat 使用资产宏提供完整的资产管道：

blocks.html_en: At build time, the Topcoat CLI collects or downloads all your assets and stores them in a single directory. As your application runs, they are served using content hashes to optimize browser caching.

sentences.en: At build time, the Topcoat CLI collects or downloads all your assets and stores them in a single directory.
sentences.zh: 在构建时，Topcoat CLI 会收集或下载您的所有资源并将它们存储在单个目录中。

sentences.en: As your application runs, they are served using content hashes to optimize browser caching.
sentences.zh: 当您的应用程序运行时，它们将使用内容哈希来优化浏览器缓存。

blocks.html_en: Every good design needs its own fonts and icons. Fontsource and Iconify offer huge libraries of freely available fonts and icons for you to use. These can be trivially included in your Topcoat app:

sentences.en: Every good design needs its own fonts and icons.
sentences.zh: 每个好的设计都需要自己的字体和图标。

sentences.en: Fontsource and Iconify offer huge libraries of freely available fonts and icons for you to use.
sentences.zh: Fontsource 和 Iconify 提供庞大的免费字体和图标库供您使用。

sentences.en: These can be trivially included in your Topcoat app:
sentences.zh: 这些可以轻松地包含在您的 Topcoat 应用程序中：

blocks.html_en: Coming up with a cohesive design system and all of its components can be challenging. Component libraries can get you up and running quickly, but often turn out to be inflexible as your design changes. Inspired by shadcn/ui, Topcoat's built-in component library is based on Tailwind and copies ready-made components directly into your source directory. This means you can modify anything you need and make the design truly your own:

sentences.en: Coming up with a cohesive design system and all of its components can be challenging.
sentences.zh: 提出一个有凝聚力的设计系统及其所有组件可能具有挑战性。

sentences.en: Component libraries can get you up and running quickly, but often turn out to be inflexible as your design changes.
sentences.zh: 组件库可以让您快速启动并运行，但随着设计的变化，组件库往往变得不灵活。

sentences.en: Inspired by shadcn/ui, Topcoat's built-in component library is based on Tailwind and copies ready-made components directly into your source directory.
sentences.zh: 受到 shadcn/ui 的启发，Topcoat 的内置组件库基于 Tailwind，并将现成的组件直接复制到您的源目录中。

sentences.en: This means you can modify anything you need and make the design truly your own:
sentences.zh: 这意味着您可以修改您需要的任何内容并使设计真正属于您自己：

blocks.html_en: Locality of behavior as the guiding principle

sentences.en: Locality of behavior as the guiding principle
sentences.zh: 以行为局部性为指导原则

blocks.html_en: Both humans and AI are better at reasoning across small regions of code. Topcoat is architected from the ground up to allow you to keep logic local and composable. We encourage, for example, letting components do their own data fetching instead of passing data down as component arguments:

sentences.en: Both humans and AI are better at reasoning across small regions of code.
sentences.zh: 人类和人工智能都更擅长在小代码区域进行推理。

sentences.en: Topcoat is architected from the ground up to allow you to keep logic local and composable.
sentences.zh: Topcoat 是从头开始构建的，可让您保持逻辑本地化和可组合性。

sentences.en: We encourage, for example, letting components do their own data fetching instead of passing data down as component arguments:
sentences.zh: 例如，我们鼓励让组件自己获取数据，而不是将数据作为组件参数向下传递：

blocks.html_en: To avoid fetching data multiple times, Topcoat has built-in request-level memoization, inspired by React's cache:

sentences.en: To avoid fetching data multiple times, Topcoat has built-in request-level memoization, inspired by React's cache:
sentences.zh: 为了避免多次获取数据，受 React 缓存的启发，Topcoat 内置了请求级记忆功能：

blocks.html_en: This principle can be extended further to authentication. Instead of relying on middleware in a completely different part of the codebase that may or may not run, you can protect your data directly in the component:

sentences.en: This principle can be extended further to authentication.
sentences.zh: 该原则可以进一步扩展到身份验证。

sentences.en: Instead of relying on middleware in a completely different part of the codebase that may or may not run, you can protect your data directly in the component:
sentences.zh: 您可以直接在组件中保护数据，而不是依赖代码库中可能运行也可能不运行的完全不同部分的中间件：

blocks.html_en: Like hooks in React (but without the dreaded rules of hooks), functions compose nicely by passing the request context (cx) around.

sentences.en: Like hooks in React (but without the dreaded rules of hooks), functions compose nicely by passing the request context (cx) around.
sentences.zh: 就像 React 中的钩子一样（但没有可怕的钩子规则），函数通过传递请求上下文（cx）来很好地组合。

blocks.html_en: What about Axum

sentences.en: What about Axum
sentences.zh: 阿克苏姆呢

blocks.html_en: Axum is another crate hosted on tokio-rs for building server applications. Because of this, I want to call out that Topcoat and Axum cover very different use cases. In fact, I expect many Topcoat users will end up using Axum as well within their projects. Axum is a lower-level HTTP router that makes it easy to build HTTP API endpoints. You could also use Axum to build a reactive web app, but it would take a lot more boilerplate and setup. Topcoat aims to remove that boilerplate, but if you just want to build those lower-level HTTP endpoints, you should still reach for Axum.

sentences.en: Axum is another crate hosted on tokio-rs for building server applications.
sentences.zh: Axum 是 tokio-rs 上托管的另一个用于构建服务器应用程序的包。

sentences.en: Because of this, I want to call out that Topcoat and Axum cover very different use cases.
sentences.zh: 因此，我想指出 Topcoat 和 Axum 涵盖了非常不同的用例。

sentences.en: In fact, I expect many Topcoat users will end up using Axum as well within their projects.
sentences.zh: 事实上，我预计许多 Topcoat 用户最终也会在他们的项目中使用 Axum。

sentences.en: Axum is a lower-level HTTP router that makes it easy to build HTTP API endpoints.
sentences.zh: Axum 是一个较低级别的 HTTP 路由器，可以轻松构建 HTTP API 端点。

sentences.en: You could also use Axum to build a reactive web app, but it would take a lot more boilerplate and setup.
sentences.zh: 您还可以使用 Axum 构建反应式 Web 应用程序，但这需要更多的样板文件和设置。

sentences.en: Topcoat aims to remove that boilerplate, but if you just want to build those lower-level HTTP endpoints, you should still reach for Axum.
sentences.zh: Topcoat 的目标是删除该样板文件，但如果您只想构建那些较低级别的 HTTP 端点，您仍然应该使用 Axum。

blocks.html_en: What next

sentences.en: What next
sentences.zh: 接下来做什么

blocks.html_en: This is just the first release of Topcoat. There is a lot on our roadmap, including stuff like tighter integration with Toasty, Validations, email, etc. You can get a sense for some of it in the Readme.

sentences.en: This is just the first release of Topcoat.
sentences.zh: 这只是 Topcoat 的第一个版本。

sentences.en: There is a lot on our roadmap, including stuff like tighter integration with Toasty, Validations, email, etc. You can get a sense for some of it in the Readme.
sentences.zh: 我们的路线图上有很多内容，包括与 Toasty、验证、电子邮件等更紧密的集成等内容。您可以在自述文件中了解其中的一些内容。

blocks.html_en: In the meantime, Topcoat is ready to use now, and if you need a database, pull Toasty. Give it a try, and send us questions and/or feedback. We are all on the #topcoat channel in the Tokio Discord.

sentences.en: In the meantime, Topcoat is ready to use now, and if you need a database, pull Toasty.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，Topcoat 现在就可以使用了，如果您需要数据库，请拉 Toasty。

sentences.en: Give it a try, and send us questions and/or feedback.
sentences.zh: 尝试一下，并向我们发送问题和/或反馈。

sentences.en: We are all on the #topcoat channel in the Tokio Discord.
sentences.zh: 我们都在 Tokio Discord 的#topcoat 频道上。

blocks.html_en: Happy building.

sentences.en: Happy building.
sentences.zh: 快乐的建筑。

content_html_zh:
Topcoat 是一个模块化、包含电池的 Rust 框架，用于构建全栈、反应式 Web 应用程序。 它优先考虑简单性和生产力。 Topcoat 完全由服务器渲染。 通过在服务器中渲染 HTML 片段并添加“反应性指令”作为元数据（与 HTMX 的思想类似）来添加反应性。

这是带有 Topcoat 的“hello world”应用程序：

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    topcoat::start(Router::builder().discover().build()).await.unwrap();
}

#[page("/")]
async fn home() -> Result {
    view! {
        <!DOCTYPE html>
        <html>
            <head>
                <title>"Hello world"</title>
                topcoat::dev::script()
            </head>
            <body>
                hello(name: "World")
            </body>
        </html>
    }
}

#[component]
async fn hello(name: &str) -> Result {
    view! {
        <h1>"Hello, " (name) "!"</h1>
    }
}

入门指南

动机

在深入了解更多技术细节之前，有必要先讨论一下 Topcoat 背后的动机。 如果你看过我的 TokioConf 演讲（我在演讲中预测 Rust 可能成为所有新领域开发的前 3 名语言），那么这一切都不会让人感到惊讶。

三年前，如果我告诉你 Rust 将是一种构建 Web 应用程序的绝佳语言，你可能会说我疯了，这是理所当然的。 毕竟，网络应用程序并不是传统上对性能敏感的应用程序。 因此，适合这项工作的工具应该是能让您快速发货的工具。 性能是可有可无的。 毫不奇怪，最丰富的 Web 应用程序生态系统都是高度关注生产力的语言，例如 JavaScript、Ruby 和 PHP。

然而，人工智能已经彻底重塑了这种计算方式。 人工智能消除了学习障碍和生产力差距。 现代编码人工智能工具构建某些东西所需的时间差异主要是可用库集的因素，而不是编程语言的因素。 甚至操作员的具体专业知识也变得不那么重要。 我见过经验丰富的软件工程师，他们从未编写过 Rust，从第一天开始就使用 AI 工具使用 Rust 进行构建。 我不是在谈论氛围编码，而是利用一个人的通用工程经验与人工智能工具交互工作，在学习语言的同时取得进步。

现在，我认为 Rust 是一种很棒的语言，它有一些非常好的特性（速度和可靠性）。 然而，这并不意味着我认为如果您已经有一个适合您的堆栈并且不需要 Rust 的性能和可靠性，您应该切换到 Rust（好吧，我确实认为您应该这样做，但我也知道这是我对 Rust 的热爱，而不是一个实际的建议）。 然而，许多组织正在采用 Rust，因为他们需要解决需要性能和可靠语言的问题。 采用 Rust 的组织已经有很多理由继续使用 Rust 来开发更高级别的应用程序，不是因为他们需要性能，而是因为他们已经拥有内部基础设施（库、构建系统、流程……） 围绕该语言，并最大限度地减少组织内的编程语言和工具的数量确实可以提高生产力。

所以现在，我们需要一个丰富的库生态系统来提高 Web 应用程序的 Rust 生产力。 这就是我一直在构建的。 我从 Toasty 开始，它是 Rust 的 ORM，因为它可能是最难的组件。 Toasty 自 2026 年 4 月起即可使用。 路线图的下一步是 Web 框架，这就是 Topcoat 的用武之地。

去年年底，我遇到了 Julien Scholz，他的品味和为 Rust 构建出色的 Web 应用程序框架的热情给我留下了深刻的印象，所以我说服他花时间构建一个：Topcoat。

没有 WebAssembly 的反应性

像 Leptos 和 Dioxus 这样的框架非常适合使用 Rust 创建高度交互的 Web 应用程序，方法是将代码编译为 WebAssembly 并在浏览器中运行。 然而，许多应用程序不需要这种级别的交互性。 对于这些用例，编译到单独的目标、担心包大小和拆分以及跨客户端/服务器边界序列化数据都成为一种负担。 Topcoat 追求更简单的方法。 所有标记都在服务器上呈现，因此组件可以异步、访问数据库或安全地验证用户权限。 为了增加反应性，它使用宏将经过完全类型检查的 Rust 表达式的子集交叉编译为 JavaScript，从而使您可以继续使用 Rust，而无需接触 WebAssembly。 在此示例中，单击 <button> 时会显示下面的 <p> 标记：

view! {
    // Declare a client-side state variable:
    signal open = false;
    
    <button
        // Configure a Rust closure as the "on click" handler for this button.
        // Code inside the $(...) is run as JavaScript inside the browser:
        @click=$(|_e| open.set(!open.get()))
    >
        "What is Topcoat?"
    </button>
    
    // The `hidden` attribute tracks the value of `open` and updates
    // each time the button is pressed.
    <p :hidden=$(!open.get())>"A fullstack Rust framework."</p>
}

切换逻辑完全在浏览器中运行，不需要服务器往返。

每当客户端状态发生变化时，Topcoat 还可以在服务器上重新渲染 UI 的整个部分，并仅交换页面的必要部分。 在这里，当用户在搜索 <input> 字段中键入内容时，搜索结果就会更新：

#[component]
async fn search() -> Result {
    view! {
        signal query = String::new();

        // Write the current text input into the `query` signal:
        <input @input=$(|e: Event| query.set(e.target.value))>

        // Updates as the user types.
        search_results(query: $(query.get()))
    }
}

// Shards are a special type of component that exposes an API endpoint from your router.
#[shard]
async fn search_results(cx: &Cx, query: String) -> Result {
    // This function runs on the server. It can access the database asynchronously.
    view! {
        <ul>
            for product in search_products(cx, &query).await? {
                <li>(product.name)</li>
            }
        </ul>
    }
}

客户端反应性系统仍处于开发的早期阶段，并且存在一些局限性。 我们有很多想法可以在未来改进这一点。 同时，您还可以利用 HTMX 和 Alpine.js 集成。

用于 UI 开发的完整工具集

构建 Web 界面比渲染 HTML 复杂得多。 Web 应用程序需要加载自定义字体、样式表、图像等。 Topcoat 使用资产宏提供完整的资产管道：

const FERRIS: Asset = asset!("./ferris.png");

view! { <img src=(FERRIS)> }

在构建时，Topcoat CLI 会收集或下载您的所有资源并将它们存储在单个目录中。 当您的应用程序运行时，它们将使用内容哈希来优化浏览器缓存。

每个好的设计都需要自己的字体和图标。 Fontsource 和 Iconify 提供庞大的免费字体和图标库供您使用。 这些可以轻松地包含在您的 Topcoat 应用程序中：

// Loads the "Roboto" web font from Fontsource.
const ROBOTO: Font = fontsource_font!(ROBOTO);

// Creates a Rust module containing the "feather" icon set.
iconify::include!("feather");

提出一个有凝聚力的设计系统及其所有组件可能具有挑战性。 组件库可以让您快速启动并运行，但随着设计的变化，组件库往往变得不灵活。 受到 shadcn/ui 的启发，Topcoat 的内置组件库基于 Tailwind，并将现成的组件直接复制到您的源目录中。 这意味着您可以修改您需要的任何内容并使设计真正属于您自己：

#[component]
async fn delete_card() -> Result {
    view! {
        card(
            card_header(
                card_title("Delete workspace")
                card_description("This permanently removes the workspace and all of its data.")
            )
            card_footer(
                attrs: attributes! { class="justify-end" },
                button(variant: ButtonVariant::Ghost, "Cancel")
                button(variant: ButtonVariant::Destructive, "Delete workspace")
            )
        )
    }
}

以行为局部性为指导原则

人类和人工智能都更擅长在小代码区域进行推理。 Topcoat 是从头开始构建的，可让您保持逻辑本地化和可组合性。 例如，我们鼓励让组件自己获取数据，而不是将数据作为组件参数向下传递：

#[component]
async fn user_profile(cx: &Cx, user_id: &str) -> Result {
    // Only this component knows what user data it needs.
    let user = load_user(cx, user_id).await?;
    view! {
        <h1>(user.name)</h1>
        ...
    }
}

为了避免多次获取数据，受 React 缓存的启发，Topcoat 内置了请求级记忆功能：

#[memoize]
async fn load_user(cx: &Cx, user_id: &str) -> Result<User> {
    // This database call is made only once per unique `user_id`.
    db(cx).load_user_by_id(user_id).await
}

该原则可以进一步扩展到身份验证。 您可以直接在组件中保护数据，而不是依赖代码库中可能运行也可能不运行的完全不同部分的中间件：

async fn require_auth(cx: &Cx) -> Result<User> {
    if let Some(Session { user_id }) = current_session(cx).await? {
        Ok(load_user(cx, user_id).await?)
    } else {
        // Data is kept secret, redirect to login.
        Err(redirect("/login").into())
    }
}

#[component]
async fn user_profile(cx: &Cx) -> Result {
    // `user_profile` protects itself from misuse if the user is not logged in!
    let user = require_auth(cx).await?;
    view! {
        <h1>(user.name)</h1>
        ...
    }
}

就像 React 中的钩子一样（但没有可怕的钩子规则），函数通过传递请求上下文（cx）来很好地组合。

阿克苏姆呢

Axum 是 tokio-rs 上托管的另一个用于构建服务器应用程序的包。 因此，我想指出 Topcoat 和 Axum 涵盖了非常不同的用例。 事实上，我预计许多 Topcoat 用户最终也会在他们的项目中使用 Axum。 Axum 是一个较低级别的 HTTP 路由器，可以轻松构建 HTTP API 端点。 您还可以使用 Axum 构建反应式 Web 应用程序，但这需要更多的样板文件和设置。 Topcoat 的目标是删除该样板文件，但如果您只想构建那些较低级别的 HTTP 端点，您仍然应该使用 Axum。

接下来做什么

这只是 Topcoat 的第一个版本。 我们的路线图上有很多内容，包括与 Toasty、验证、电子邮件等更紧密的集成等内容。您可以在自述文件中了解其中的一些内容。

与此同时，Topcoat 现在就可以使用了，如果您需要数据库，请拉 Toasty。 尝试一下，并向我们发送问题和/或反馈。 我们都在 Tokio Discord 的#topcoat 频道上。

快乐的建筑。
